fab_section_md:
- Baelon the Brave, "the Spring Prince," was fourteen years older than Viserra and widowed of his sister Alyssa (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p04:73)
- When Aemon was slain he came to Tarth on Vhagar with Dark Sister and slaughtered the Myrish (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p04:197)
- He became Prince of Dragonstone and Hand of the King and remained near the queen to the end (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p04:245)
- Alysanne told him (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p04:245)
identity_line: Baelon Targaryen — Baelon the Brave, "the Spring Prince," was fourteen years older than Viserra and widowed of his sister Alyssa.

fab_section_md:
- Prince Baelon's sixteen-year-old son Viserys entered the Dragonpit in 93 AC and claimed the aged Balerion (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p04:211)
- He flew thrice around the city but judged the Black Dread too weak to reach Dragonstone (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p04:211)
identity_line: Viserys Targaryen — Prince Baelon's sixteen-year-old son Viserys entered the Dragonpit in 93 AC and claimed the aged Balerion.

fab_section_md:
- Balerion, the Black Dread, had stopped growing and grown sluggish; claimed by Viserys, he died less than a year later (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p04:213)
- Septon Barth wrote of him (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p04:213)
identity_line: Balerion, the Black Dread, had stopped growing and grown sluggish; claimed by Viserys, he died less than a year later.
